overview.Summary: A cyber attack targeting a healthcare organization through a phishing campaign leading to data destruction and encryption.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Motivation: Disruption of healthcare services and potential financial gain through ransomware.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2016-7855 (Suggested): Adobe Flash Player Memory Corruption Vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: Targets mobile users with phishing attacks to solicit sensitive information.
stage.description: The attack begins with a phishing email containing malicious links or attachments exploiting CVE-2016-7855, compromising internet-facing assets through web-based phishing attempts.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-41379 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows Print Spooler Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 167 Name: White Box Reverse Engineering Description: Involves analyzing software executables to understand their structure and function, enabling unauthorized access.
stage.description: After gaining initial access via phishing, a reverse shell is established to execute arbitrary commands remotely, maintaining persistence and control over the compromised system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675 Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices Description: Acquiring data from decommissioned systems to extract sensitive information.
stage.description: The attackers encrypt or destroy critical healthcare data stored on the system, disrupting operations and forcing financial recovery efforts.

Scenario.summary: A sophisticated phishing attack targeting a healthcare organization leads to remote system compromise and subsequent data encryption, causing operational disruptions and financial loss.
